features.featureTitle: In frontend/src/pages/DayTracker.tsx, add a prominent statistics summary panel at the top of the page (above the entry list) that displays four metrics computed from the user's day tracker entries stored in localStorage: (1) 'All Days Hours' — the total sum of all logged hours across every date ever recorded, (2) 'This Week Hours' — the sum of logged hours for entries whose date falls within the current calendar week (Monday to Sunday), (3) 'Today Hours' — the sum of logged hours for entries whose date matches today's ISO date, and (4) 'Today Minutes' — the equivalent of today's total hours expressed in minutes (e.g., 2.5 hours → 150 minutes). Display each metric in its own card with a clear label and a large numeric value. All calculations must react to changes in the entries data (i.e., update immediately when entries are added, edited, or deleted). Use date-fns or dayjs (already present in the project) for date comparisons and week boundary calculations.
